items.front: What governs ATC service in oceanic controlled airspace?
items.back: Use the procedures in Chapter 8 unless a directive or letter of agreement prescribes different procedures or minima.

items.front: How is ATC service provided in offshore airspace areas?
items.back: Use Chapter 8 procedures and minima, and when Chapter 8 does not cover the situation, apply the rest of the Order.

items.front: When are VFR flight plans permitted in oceanic FIRs?
items.back: Only between sunrise and sunset, only in the listed oceanic areas, and only when basic VFR weather minimums are met.

items.front: What types of separation are approved in Chapter 8?
items.back: Vertical separation, horizontal separation by longitudinal or lateral means, or radar separation where radar coverage is adequate.

items.front: What altimeter setting basis is used for altitude assignment in oceanic control areas?
items.back: Use flight levels and a standard altimeter setting of 29.92 unless directed or charted otherwise.

items.front: When a position report affecting separation is missing in oceanic operations, how soon must action be taken?
items.back: Take action to obtain the report no later than 10 minutes after the control estimate unless otherwise specified.

items.front: Why are oceanic gross-navigation, height, time, and SAO verification errors reported?
items.back: They are reported under JO 7210.632 so the data can support risk modeling and separation-standard reduction work.

items.front: What are control estimates used for in oceanic ATC?
items.back: They are time-based estimates of aircraft position, updated by automation or the controller, and they must be used when applying time-based separation minima.

items.front: When may a non-RVSM aircraft be allowed in RVSM oceanic airspace?
items.back: Only if it qualifies as an excepted aircraft and is previously approved by the operations supervisor or CIC.

items.front: What is the ATC sequence for an oceanic weather-deviation request when conflicting traffic exists horizontally?
items.back: Give weather deviations priority, establish vertical separation first, then issue the deviation clearance. If separation cannot be established, advise conflicting traffic and request the pilot's intentions.
